page_content:
Prerequisite: AAAS101.

Recommended: Completion of one semester of statistics is recommended.

Credit Only Granted for: AASP301 or AAAS301.

Formerly: AASP301.

AAAS306 Race, Gender and Artificial Intelligence (Al) (3 Credits)

With the recent rise of accessible Al technologies, concerns about not only the potential flaws built into technology but also the impact of those issues on already marginalized communities loom large. From medical applications of Al in the personalizing of treatment and disease diagnosis, to the use of facial recognition software in the criminal justice system, Al is being integrated into all facets of everyday life. This class will explore the intersection of Artificial Intelligence (Al), race, and gender. There will be assigned readings on race, gender, difference, algorithms, and artificial intelligence. Students will engage with Al platforms, such as ChatGPT or Peo to gain hands-on experience about the platforms and how they work.
